title: vicia cassubica var. orobus
intro: Vicia cassubica var. orobus, also known as the lesser bird's foot trefoil, is a plant species within the pea family (Fabaceae) that is native to Europe and parts of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues such as diarrhea and constipation, due to its anthelmintic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Vicia cassubica var. orobus include its ability to soothe and calm the digestive system, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a variety of applications.
